question.label: Next-step focus
question.name: Given what we’ve covered, what is the one change you want to make first?
question.description: Closes on a concrete priority and commitment from the conversation.

answer: I will choose one recurring deliverable and define what good looks like before the next review cycle.

assessment.note: She named a specific behavioural change and timing — choosing a recurring deliverable and defining what good looks like before the next review cycle — which is a positive commitment on clarity and growth.

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.
That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I want to lead one cross-team decision this cycle, end to end.
Specific feedback checkpoints during projects would help me level up faster.
I will choose one recurring deliverable and define what good looks like before the next review cycle.
